additionalGuardrails: Avoid framing relationship expectations as diagnoses, verdicts, or hard compatibility judgments.
Keep conflict, trust, and boundaries language especially tentative unless both payloads are clearly stable.

showdown.reason: Relationship expectations should stay in a private one-to-one compare flow, not a group summary.

warnings.readiness: This deck covers relationship expectations and needs stronger confidence, coverage, and stability before compare should unlock.
warnings.consent: This deck touches private expectations, boundaries, and trust. Keep the compare intentional, in person, and explicitly consented.
warnings.report: Relationship preference output should stay cautious and specific to this deck. It should not turn private expectations into sweeping judgments.

tag_rules.note: Boundary signal should be withheld until it has clear, stable evidence.
Conflict preferences can be socially risky when they are still ambiguous.
Trust themes need stable evidence before they should appear in compare output.

additionalGuardrails: Values should be described as current deck-specific signal, not as a full moral or ideological portrait.
When worldviews diverge, frame the difference as a conversation topic rather than a verdict.

showdown.reason: Values decks are too sensitive for a playful group showdown mode.

warnings.readiness: This deck touches worldview and value priorities, so compare needs stronger confidence and lower ambiguity than a standard deck.
warnings.consent: Value differences can feel personal quickly. Keep this compare intentional, local, and focused on discussion rather than judgment.
warnings.report: Value summaries should stay uncertainty-aware and avoid turning partial signal into a fixed worldview label.

tag_rules.note: Family priorities should not be surfaced when the signal is still unstable.
Justice themes can overstate worldview differences if confidence is weak.
Tradition themes need extra care and should not drive direct prompts when uncertainty remains.

additionalGuardrails: Avoid framing communication signal as a diagnosis or permanent flaw.
Keep emotional openness and avoidance themes especially tentative unless both payloads are stable.

showdown.reason: Communication style should stay in a one-to-one compare flow instead of a group showdown.

warnings.readiness: Communication style compare needs stronger stability because tone and context can distort early signal.
warnings.consent: This deck can surface vulnerable themes about conflict and openness. Keep the compare careful, explicit, and non-performative.
warnings.report: Communication summaries should stay deck-scoped and avoid pathologizing either person.

tag_rules.note: Vulnerability signal should not be exported until it is stable.
Passive communication themes should stay cautious and avoid direct prompts.

additionalGuardrails: If boundaries appear, keep the tone careful and avoid implying one right social style.

warnings.consent: Some social-habit themes, especially around boundaries, may be withheld until the signal is stronger.
warnings.report: If social boundary themes remain uncertain, the report should withhold them rather than speculate.

tag_rules.note: Boundary themes inside an otherwise standard deck still need extra care.
